title: Korean Egg Toast

overview.description: A popular Korean street-food breakfast of fluffy vegetable omelette in butter-toasted bread with a sweet and savoury finish.

ingredients: 4 slices thick milk bread (or substitute white bread)
method: Stir in the cabbage, carrot and spring onions.
Season lightly with salt and black pepper.
Melt half the butter in a frying pan over a medium heat.
Pour in half the egg mixture and cook until just set.
Fold into a rectangle roughly the size of the bread.
Repeat with the remaining egg mixture.
Toast the bread in the remaining butter until golden.
Place an omelette on each slice of toast and top with the cheese.
Top with the remaining toast and serve immediately.

learning.did-you-know: Korean Egg Toast became famous through street-food vendors such as Isaac Toast, where the signature sweet sprinkle of sugar balances the savoury filling.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How finely shredded vegetables make an omelette lighter and sweeter while adding texture.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A quick breakfast or lunch using everyday ingredients that's likely to appeal to both adults and children.
chapman-tips: Shred the cabbage as finely as possible so it softens quickly.
Don't skip the tiny sprinkle of sugar—it balances the flavours rather than making the toast sweet.
Cook the omelette gently so it stays fluffy.
Thick-cut bread gives the best texture.
